chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. B.A. No. S- 897 of 2015 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 11.03.2016 1. For Orders on Office Objection 2. For Hearing Mr. Ghulam Asghar Mirbahar, Advocate for applicant Mr. Riazat Ali Saahar, Advocate for complainant Syed Meeral Shah, D.P.G. Applicant is accused in Crime No. 80 of 2014 registered at police station Dharo district Shaheed Benazirabad under Section 302, 337-J, and 34 PPC. The allegations against the applicant are that he along with co-accused strangulated his wife namely Mst. Neelam daughter of complainant in his house on 10.8.2014 at 0800 hours. The post mortem report supports the allegations levelled against the applicant that the deceased died unnatural death due to asphyxia. It is obvious that in the FIR direct allegations of causing murder of deceased are levelled against the applicant. His counsel has argued that the case against him is one of further inquiry. However, in presence of such direct allegations I cannot accede to such arguments. Mr. Riazat Ali Saahar, appearing on behalf of complainant has placed on record the case diaries showing that on each and every date of hearing the P.Ws are appearing in the court but the accused on one pretext or the other are not proceeding with the matter. In the circumstances, while dismissing the bail application I would direct the Trial Court to expedite the trial and record the evidence of material witnesses preferably within a period of three months. In the above terms, bail application is disposed of. JUDGE *Karar/-
